labs.maintenanceFrequency: every 3-6 months when eGFR <60 ml/min per 1.73m²
labs.actionRequired: Dose adjustment when eGFR <45; discontinue when eGFR <30
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor kidney function to prevent lactic acidosis risk due to impaired drug excretion
Metformin interferes with intestinal vitamin B12 absorption

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Kidney transplant recipients: treat according to same eGFR recommendations as CKD patients. Dose adjustment required for eGFR <45; discontinue for eGFR <30
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: Monitor eGFR in patients treated with metformin. Increase the frequency of monitoring when the eGFR is <60 ml/min per 1.73m². Adjust the dose of metformin when the eGFR is <45 ml/min per 1.73m², and for some patients when the eGFR is 45–59 ml/min per 1.73m². Monitor patients for vitamin B12 deficiency when they are treated with metformin for more than 4 years.

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
